name: Deepa Krishnan
summary: Personalized 12-step study plan targeting 3 weak areas with progressive difficulty.

steps.description: Theory explanation and conceptual understanding for a topic
steps.reasoning: Laws of Motion has very low average marks (5.0). Start with conceptual understanding before attempting problems.
steps.message_to_student: Let's begin by strengthening your fundamentals in Laws of Motion. We'll go through the key concepts and theory so you have a solid foundation before practice.

steps.description: Formula revision sheet for specified chapters
steps.reasoning: Revise key formulas for Laws of Motion before practice to ensure quick recall during problem-solving.
steps.message_to_student: Time to review the important formulas for Laws of Motion. Having these at your fingertips will help you solve problems faster.

steps.description: Targeted practice set for specific topics, no timer
steps.reasoning: Start with easier problems in Laws of Motion to build confidence. No timer to reduce pressure.
steps.message_to_student: Now let's practice Laws of Motion with some problems. Take your time — there's no timer. Focus on understanding each solution.

steps.target_chapter: Laws of Motion, Chemical Bonding, Thermodynamics
steps.reasoning: A structured 3-day revision plan covering all weak areas (Laws of Motion, Chemical Bonding, Thermodynamics) for systematic improvement.
steps.message_to_student: Here's a 3-day revision plan covering Laws of Motion, Chemical Bonding, Thermodynamics. Follow it daily for consistent improvement.

steps.description: Full timed mock test simulating exam conditions
steps.reasoning: A timed mock test to assess improvement on weak chapters under exam conditions.
steps.message_to_student: Time to test yourself! This timed mock will show how much you've improved on your weak areas. Treat it like a real exam.

steps.reasoning: End with a confidence-building speed race in Organic Chemistry — a strong area — to maintain motivation.
steps.message_to_student: Great work! Let's end with a fun challenge — race against a bot in Organic Chemistry, one of your strongest topics!
